title: cerasus avium subsp. duracina
intro: Cerasus avium subsp. duracina, also known as wild cherry, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of consuming wild cherry include relief from arthritis, fever, and coughs, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of wild cherry include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation.
